title: Calycogonium tetragonolobum

The classification of Calycogonium tetragonolobum also includes its classification as a species within the genus Calycogonium. intro: Calycogonium tetragonolobum, also known as Puerto Rican calyco, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Calycogonium tetragonolobum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects.
